summary: The 5%ers scored 78/100 in our 2026 real-money stress test. They aren't the flashiest prop firm out there, and their manual backend processes can be slow, but their trading conditions are elite. During our live spread test, USD/JPY held steady at 0.0 pips, and even during a US unemployment claims release, spreads only widened to 1 pip — while other firms blew out to 4+ pips. The scaling plan is arguably the best in the industry: hit specific milestones and your account doubles, eventually reaching $4 million with a 100% profit split and a monthly salary of up to $10,000. If you value tight spreads and a clear path to scaling over a fancy dashboard, The 5%ers are a top-tier choice.
pros: Elite spread stability — 0.0 pips on USD/JPY in testing
$4 per round turn FX commissions (vs $7 industry avg)
cons: Dashboard P&L doesn't update in real-time
News trading rules manually enforced with delays (up to a week)
Bootcamp requires mandatory stop-loss on every trade
Profit split starts at 50% on Bootcamp/Hypergrowth
bestFor: Experienced traders who value institutional-grade spreads, a structured scaling path to $4M, and the rare perk of a monthly salary — especially intraday scalpers who need tight spreads during news events
notIdealFor: Traders who want a slick, real-time dashboard experience or who rely heavily on bracket strategies around news releases. Also not ideal if you need a high profit split from day one (Bootcamp starts at 50%)

categorySections.challenges: The 5%ers offer three distinct evaluation models: Bootcamp (3-step, low-cost entry from $95), High Stakes (2-step, 1:100 leverage, from $39), and Hyper Growth (1-step with account doubling, from $260). Each caters to different risk appetites. The scaling plan is exceptional — accounts can reach $4M with a 100% profit split, and traders receive monthly salaries at higher funding tiers ($4K at $350K, $10K at $500K). The 5%ers scored 8/10 for challenges.
categorySections.spreads: We scored The 5%ers 8/10 for pricing. FX commissions are $4 per round turn — significantly cheaper than the $7 industry average. During our live test, USD/JPY held at 0.0 pips, and during a US unemployment claims release, spreads only widened to 1 pip while competitors blew out to 4+ pips. Indices have zero commission. Metals, crypto, and commodities use a percentage-based commission formula. The 5%ers scored 8/10 for spreads.
categorySections.markets: The 5%ers offer CFDs across five asset classes: FX, Metals, Indices, Crypto, and Other Commodities. Leverage varies significantly by program — High Stakes offers up to 1:100 on FX, while Bootcamp and Hyper Growth max out at 1:30. Crypto leverage is limited (1:2 on High Stakes, 1:0.6 on others). No consistency rules are applied. The 5%ers scored 7/10 for markets.
categorySections.platforms: The 5%ers support MetaTrader 5 and MatchTrader. US-based clients typically must use MatchTrader due to MetaQuotes restrictions. During testing, the dashboard P&L didn't update in real-time — often showing profit when MT5 showed drawdown. Always rely on your trading terminal as the source of truth. The 5%ers scored 7/10 for platforms.
categorySections.payouts: Bootcamp and Hyper Growth start with a 50% profit split, scaling to 100%. High Stakes starts at 80%, also scaling to 100%. Minimum payout is $150, processed bi-weekly. Withdrawal methods include Rise (2% fee), Crypto (2% fee), Bank Transfer (3% fee), and HUB Credits (no fee). The 5%ers scored 8/10 for payouts.
categorySections.support: The 5%ers provide support during business hours via live chat and email, with an active Discord community. While not 24/7, the support team is responsive to account and platform issues. Educational resources are available through the community channels.
categorySections.trust: The 5%ers hold a 4.9/5 TrustPilot rating — exceptionally high for the prop firm industry. Founded in 2016, they are one of the longest-running prop firms. They use commercial liquidity providers, which generally results in smoother fills and less 'stop-hunting.' The 50,000+ trader community and consistent payout history add to their credibility. The 5%ers scored 9/10 for trust.

verdictBlocks.heading: Final Verdict: 78/100
verdictBlocks.body:
The 5%ers are a "trader's prop firm." They aren't the flashiest, and their manual backend processes can be slow, but their trading conditions are elite. If you value tight spreads and a clear path to scaling over a fancy dashboard, they are a top-tier choice for 2026.

The scaling plan is genuinely exceptional — not just because of the $4M ceiling, but because of the salary component at $350K and $500K funding levels. Few firms in the industry offer this kind of safety net for funded traders.

The main drawbacks are the dashboard lag (always rely on MT5 for real-time P&L) and the manual enforcement of news trading rules, which caused a profitable trade of mine to be disqualified nearly a week after the fact. These backend issues are the primary reason the overall score sits at 78 rather than higher.

For experienced traders who can manage their risk, understand the rules, and don't need a polished UI to feel confident, The 5%ers deliver where it matters most: execution, spreads, and a genuine path to professional-level funding.

faq.a: Yes, automated trading and EAs are allowed on all account types, provided they are not used for arbitrage or high-frequency botting that exploits the demo environment. The EA must set a Stop Loss on every position and must not copy trades from other persons' signals, perform tick scalping, or use emulators. Accounts found using EAs that violate these rules will be canceled, banned, and not refunded.

faq.q: Can I trade news at The 5%ers?
faq.a: Yes, news trading is permitted on Hyper Growth and Bootcamp programs, but bracket strategies (placing pending orders on both sides of a news release) are prohibited. On the High Stakes program, executing orders 2 minutes before until 2 minutes after high-impact news is prohibited. Be aware that news trading rules may be manually enforced with delays.

On the Hyper Growth and Bootcamp plans, every time you hit a 5–10% profit target (depending on the stage), the firm doubles your capital. Once you hit the $2.5M mark, your profit share moves to 100%. At $350K funding, you start receiving a $4,000 fixed monthly salary. At $500K, that increases to $10,000/month. The maximum account size is $4 million.
